subject: Review C-DRP2.3: unnamed
body:
## Claim Review Request: C-DRP2.3
**Paper:** DRP-2 — Testability as a Relational Property: Epistemological Consequences of the Upstream Measurement Principle
**Claim:** unnamed
**Type:** F

### Statement
AI systems trained exclusively on standard-format scientific prose perform at chance on distinguishing vacuous from non-vacuous falsification predicates.

### Falsification Predicate
**FALSIFIED_IF:** accuracy_prose_trained > 0.70
**WHERE:** [{'name': 'accuracy_prose_trained', 'type': 'scalar', 'units': 'dimensionless', 'source': 'accuracy of a prose-trained AI system on a balanced benchmark of 200+ vacuous vs non-vacuous predicates, where vacuous predicates have O=f(P) and non-vacuous predicates have O independent of P; surface-feature-controlled per §4.2.1 benchmark protocol'}]
**EVALUATION:** construct benchmark per §4.2.1 specification; evaluate prose-trained system; measure accuracy against ground truth (κ >= 0.80 inter-rater threshold); compare to 0.70 threshold; finite
**BOUNDARY:** accuracy = 0.70 → FALSIFIED (inclusive; 0.70 threshold = 20pp above chance = minimum evidence of structural capability beyond pattern-matching)
**CONTEXT:** C-DRP2.3 · PRIOR PREDICTION: prose training provides no structural signal for O-from-P detection; expected accuracy ~0.50 + noise · threshold 0.70: 20pp above chance chosen as minimum evidence of structural capability

### Requested
1. Verify the falsification predicate is logically consistent
2. Check math against known results (Strogatz, Kuznetsov, etc.)
3. Identify counterexamples or edge cases missed
4. Cross-reference against published literature
5. Provide verdict with confidence and reasoning
